rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARTHA H. EGGELING EXEMPT TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Order, And For Entry Of Statement Of Decision Before Re Entry Of Order On Petition For Allowance Of Compensation To Former Trustee And Counsel
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Harms' unopposed request for judicial notice is GRANTED. Notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in pleadings or transcripts. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Suzanne C. Farnocchia and Martha L. Eggeling's Motion to Set Aside Order, and for Entry of Statement of Decision Before Re-entry of Order on Petition for Allowance of Compensation to Former Trustee and Counsel is DENIED. There was not a trial nor an evidentiary hearing in this case. Both parties expressly stated that they did not want a trial or evidentiary hearing, as is reflected in the minutes of the hearing. The parties submitted on the papers, and the court decided the dispute based solely on the papers submitted by the parties. No evidence was taken in open court, and neither a trial nor an evidentiary hearing was held. Assuming, arguendo, that the matter being called on the morning probate calendar somehow qualified as a trial, that does not mean that a statement of decision was required. Both CCP Section 632 and CRC 3.1590(n) provide that if a trial is completed within one day or in less than eight hours over more than one day, a request for statement of decision must be made before the matter is submitted. In this case, no request for a statement of decision was made prior to the matter being submitted on October 13, 2021. Farnocchia and Eggeling's request for relief under CCP Section 473 is DENIED. The mandatory provision under CCP Section 473 only applies to a default judgment or dismissal. (See English v. IKON Business Solutions, Inc. (2001) 94 Ca.App.4th 130, 143, 145-146, 148-149.) Here an order was entered after full briefing and oral argument. The order issued by the court was not a default judgment or a dismissal. Further, Farnocchia and Eggeling have not requested, nor has the Court found grounds for, discretionary relief under CCP Section 473. Petitioner John T. Harms shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.
